name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: I Love My Amma - Book Launch
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for a warm and evocative discussion on I Love My Amma, a tender, funny, and deeply moving tribute to mothers and the complex bonds we share with them. First published in Kannada, this unforgettable collection of essays traces a son’s journey from childhood reverence to adult understanding, as he comes to see his mother as she truly is—flawed, stubborn, fierce, and magnificently human. Rich with vivid, everyday details, the book brings alive the chaos, humour, and quiet resilience that shape a life under Amma’s watchful presence.
Author Vasudhendra will be in conversation with translator Narayan Shankaran on 15th May, from 6 PM onwards. Together, they will reflect on the making of the book, the process of bringing it into English, and the emotional landscape of writing about family, memory, and care.
A celebrated Kannada writer and founder of Chanda Pustaka, Vasudhendra has spent decades shaping contemporary Kannada literature, while Narayan Shankaran brings his rich experience as a writer and translator to this collaboration. Their conversation promises thoughtful insights into language, translation, and the universality of intimate, personal stories.
Join us for an evening of warmth, humour, and heartfelt reflection on the relationships that shape us.

name: Lose Ctrl + Art + Create - Journaling Workshop
description:
A slightly questionable name? Yes. But that’s exactly the point.
This Friday evening is all about letting go, losing control of your thoughts, skipping the deep conversations, dropping the judgment, and allowing a little absolute nonsense.
We’ll journal like we don’t care, with fun, slightly twisted prompts that help you loosen up and just be. Whether you journal every day or once in a while, this space is especially for you.
All materials will be provided; just bring yourself and your unfiltered thoughts.
Age: 16+

name: ALT EFF Film Club x Atta Galatta - Movie Screening
description:
Join us for a punchy lineup of 4 short films (2 Indian and 2 international), each bringing its own flavour of environmental storytelling.
And then… stay for ALT EFF Voices, our signature open mic where audience members share short, personal, environment-related stories. No practice needed. Just pick a prompt from a hat and dive in.
SHORT FILM PROGRAMME:
A brilliant selection of cinematic, poetic, diverse pieces of environmental storytelling that are short enough to get you hooked and never long enough to bore!
Some Thoughts on the Common Toad
The Feast
The Butterfly Man
The Floating world
To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.
All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.
What's Included?

Exclusive screenings of the world's best environmental cinema
Access to a community of environmentally inclined, film-loving people
A chance to express yourself at ALT EFF Voices
Post-film discussions.

Student Discount
50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).
CODE: ALTSTUDENT
ABOUT ALT EFF Film Club
If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.
All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.
It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.
With snacks. Always snacks.
What's included

Environmental Film Club
Cinema
Community Building
Sustainable Living
Film Screening
Light Refreshments

Age: 18+

name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: The Hero With Many Faces - An Immersive Storytelling Experience
description:
Inspired by the work of Joseph Campbell, Amulya S Pramod and Aniket S Sharma, in collaboration with Parth Prakashan, present The Hero With Many Faces—an immersive storytelling experience exploring what truly makes a hero.
Journeying through mythology, history, and contemporary life, the event moves beyond power to uncover the many forms of strength—courage, wisdom, truth, perseverance, and self-belief. Through engaging narratives and reflections, participants discover how these timeless qualities exist not just in legends, but in everyday life.
Designed for young minds and families, this bilingual (Kannada & English) experience inspires participants to see challenges differently—and discover the hero within.
Venue: Atta Galatta Date: Saturday, 16 May 2026 | 11:30 AM
Age: All | Free Entry for kids below 12 yrs

name: Improv Comics For An Absurd World - A Comics Making Workshop
description:
Come discover how comics can be absurd, funny, odd, wise, and a great medium for self-expression!
In this fun, collaborative comics-making workshop, participants will take inspiration from Aditi's graphic novel --The Complete Shampoo and Daddy!, a tale of two friends navigating the anxieties and awkwardness of everyday existence -- to create their own short comics. Aditi will also be signing copies of her book and answering questions about comics, life, and anything else you'd like to ask her about. No prior knowledge of comics, expertise in drawing or even story ideas are necessary and all materials will be provided. 
Bonus: You'll also have the chance to find out more about independent publishing from Ashwitha Jayakumar, co-founder of Indian Summer Press, Aditi's publisher and partner-in-facilitation of the workshop.
Age: 16+

name: AG Lit Sisters: The Divakaruni Diaries - Thematic Book Club
description:
This month’s pick feels a little special 💛
We are reading Chitra Banerjee Divakaruni and honestly, her stories have a way of making you pause and think, “wait, why was her voice never heard like this before?”
It is mythology, but softer. More human. More real.
Come hang out with us, talk books, share your take, or just listen and soak it all in. No pressure to be “well-read”, just show up.
 
16 May 1:30 PM Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
Free entry. Just bring yourself.
Question of the day Which character stayed with you long after you finished the book
Keywords books, honest, women, stories, real, feelings, conversations, comfort, community, reading
Age: 18+

name: Poetry Writing Workshop - Workshop
description:
Each one of us has our own insight into what constitutes good poetry. While we accept that creative expression is spontaneous, we also believe that poetry writing is a skill which can be acquired with learning and bettered with practice. To be able to achieve this, we will approach poetry through different perspectives: of sound, rhythm, imagery, among others.
During the workshop, we will together explore poetry written by the most celebrated poets of past and present, and inspect their craft. We will also get our hands dirty, attempting newer poems during the course of the workshop. 
The workshop will broadly cover - 
1. Basics of poetry writing – the power of words, self-expression, being original, recounting and dwelling on one’s own past experience
2. Tools of poetry - rhythm and sound, imagery, metaphor, and symbols. Rhetoric, personification, simile, figures of speech.
3. Reading of popular works of poetry from India and across the world
4. Exercise to encourage participants to apply the concepts learnt and explore their own skills at writing and expression.
Age: 16+

name: Alfaz-e-Andaaz - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This event is a blend of Hindi, Urdu poetry and literature,
where you all are heartily welcomed into the wonderful world of words.
Date & Time: May 16, 2026, from 4:30 PM to 5:30 PM
Free Entry
Location: Atta Galatta, 1st Stage Indiranagar, Bangalore
Organizer: Shelly Aggarwal (Hindi Author)

Age: 16+

name: Diss A Book - Book Club
description:
Everyone talks about books they love.
We’re here for the ones that wasted our time.
If you’ve ever wanted to throw a book out the window, felt alone in a sea of 5-star reviews, or kept reading in the hope it would get better but it never did, then come diss a book with us.
Here, we unpack the hype. We dissect the flaws. We read the worst lines out loud. We debate what could have saved it. And yes, we absolutely ask, “Did we all read the same thing?”
This month we will be discussing: Romance in books
Let's find out whether the story could’ve been saved… or if it was doomed from the very first “he growled."
Come join us at Atta Galatta, on 16th May, at 5 PM.

name: Dance Movement Therapy - Workshop
description:
Not a dancer? Perfect. This space isn’t about performance—it’s about feeling.
Come explore how your body holds emotions, stories, and energy through simple, guided movement. Move, pause, breathe, express—and maybe discover something new about yourself along the way.
In this session, you’ll:
 Tune into your body & breath
 Express without words
 Let go of stress through movement
 Feel lighter, freer, more connected
No dance experience required 
Just an open mind and willingness to explore yourselves through your bodies.
Age: 15+

name: Humzubaan - Hindi & Urdu poetry meet
description:
Humzubaan, conceived by Siddharth Surana is an effort to bring together Hindi/Urdu literature enthusiasts together to celebrate the beauty of poetry. The next meet up is happening on Saturday, 16th May, 6 PM at Atta Galatta.
Recite your own or your favourite author’s poems or simply soak in the magic of poetry. Click Here to register as audience or performer. Performance Submissions are open till 14th May.
We will inform you if your poem is selected for the meet. Even if your poem is not selected, you’re most welcome to attend as an audience.
Please note:
1. The poems can be your own original work or of another author. You need to mention clearly and send in your entry in the above-mentioned form.
2. Poems/songs should be in Hindi/Urdu only.
3. If you’re planning to perform with music/props, please mention in the form. You would need to arrange for the instruments and set-up at the venue.
About the host:
Siddharth is founder of AgriCentral, a digital platform serving 11 million farmers. He writes in Hindi, Urdu and English-mostly poems, short stories and essays but with long breaks. He is also an avid quizzer, trekker and a national-level gold medallist archer.

Age: 4+

name: Get Litt Storytelling Event - A Storytelling session
description:
Event 1: Cow Says - Paws, Claws, and Oinks: A Multi-Sensory Animal Tale!
Author: Nandini Nayar
Illustrator: Malvika Nair
Step into the whimsical world of Cow Says! Kids will giggle, jump, and clap as they explore the delightful sounds of animals through an interactive storytelling session. From mooing cows, piggy oinks to roaring lions, the fun never stops! This interactive session is packed with exciting hands-on and immersive activities, promising plenty of giggles, surprises, and a moo-tastic good time!
 
Event 2: I Wish! I Wish! - A Desert Dreaming Story Adventure in the Thar!
Author: Nandini Nayar
Illustrator: Lydia Eliza
Join Little Lizard in the golden sands of the Thar Desert! Kids will be swept into a magical storytelling session as they experience Little Lizard’s adventures - filled with soaring birds, leaping gazelles, swirling desert winds and big, heartfelt wishes. Through a joyful, immersive art-and-craft activity, children will explore what it means to celebrate themselves just as they are. embracing the session’s heartwarming theme of self-acceptance and being comfortable in your own skin. A reflective, creative, and utterly fun desert escapade awaits!
Age: 3+

name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: Peel and Play - Stickers Making Workshop
description:
To all the art and craft lovers, do you hoard stickers like a maniac? Because I definitely do. But what if we could actually make more of them, and keep experimenting without holding back?
In this workshop, we’ll explore 2–3 fun and simple ways of creating your own stickers using playful prompts. Everything you need will be provided, so you can just show up and create.
And if there’s even a tiny voice in your head saying you might be “too old” for this, ignore it. This is for everyone, especially you. There’s no age limit to loving stickers.
Age: 10+

name: Come Sit With Us: A Connection Circle | Edition 2 - A Meetup
description:
Because stories of separation, healing & becoming are not heard often enough.
Because our last edition taught us they need to come out into the open.
Because we need more spaces, intimate gatherings, where real personal journeys are shared.
What to Expect
• Curated personal story readouts (anonymous or named)
• Reflections and insights from different perspectives
• A space for the conversations that the stories brought on - unstructured, unfiltered - a space to connect, talk, or simply be.
This is not a panel or a lecture but a space to listen, relate, and connect—at your own pace.
Why This Space
Because separation and relationship transitions are rarely talked about honestly and without judgment.
Because stories reduce isolation.
Because healing doesn’t always need fixing—sometimes it needs witnessing.
Do You Want to Share Your Story?
You’re invited to submit a personal story around marriage, separation, divorce, or emotional transition here.
Some selected stories will be read aloud during the gathering.
With consent, all submitted stories will also be shared on our Substack (anonymously, if requested).
Whether you’re navigating this personally, witnessing it in close circles, or engaging with it academically, we're pulling up a chair for you. Will you come sit with us?
Age: 18+

name: Why Did You Do It Like That? - A Dialogue with the Authors of And How Do You Feel About That?
description:
And How Do You Feel About That? is a book that brings together fifty stories based on real clinical work. 
It was written with the intention of demystifying psychotherapy—offering readers a glimpse of what actually happens in the conversation between therapist and client. It is written in a simple, conversational style that the layperson can relate with, but has a richness and depth that those in the world of therapy will uniquely appreciate.
Through these vignettes, the book also showcases the range and depth of Transactional Analysis psychotherapy and how its ideas come alive in real clinical encounters.
In this two-hour session, the authors, Aruna Gopakumar and Yashodhara Lal,  invite therapists, counsellors, coaches, and other helping professionals into a conversation about the thinking behind these stories. Participants who have read the book are especially welcome to bring their curiosity and questions: Why did you intervene in that moment? Tell us about your thinking here or tell us more about this technique. 
During the session, they will read short sections from selected stories and pause to unpack the therapeutic decisions within them. The authors will reflect on their learning, demonstrate selected methods, and discuss what it means to work relationally with clients.
Rather than a lecture, the evening is intended as a dialogue among practitioners interested in the craft of therapy. It is also an opportunity to meet and connect with other therapists on a relaxed Sunday evening.
Practitioners of all modalities, all experience levels welcome. Participants who have read the book will likely find the discussion particularly enriching.
Age: 18+

name: The Atta Galatta Story: A Fireside Chat with Subodh Sankar - A conversation on building a bookstore, community & culture
description:
From a beloved corner in Koramangala to a sprawling cultural landmark in Indiranagar, Atta Galatta has become synonymous with Bangalore’s literary and artistic soul. But how does one transform a passion for books into a sustainable, thriving community hub?
Join us for an exclusive evening as Sathwik NN (Founder of BFC & Co-founder of Aatmeeya) sits down with Subodh Sankar, the visionary co-founder of Atta Galatta, to peel back the curtain on the business and heart behind the brand.
---
What We’ll Explore:
● The Art of Building a Bookstore: We’ll trace the genesis of Atta Galatta—from the initial spark of an idea to the realities of the journey. Subodh will share the hopes that fueled the start and the artistic choices that give the space its unique identity.
● The Science Behind Creating a Community: A bookstore is more than just shelves; it’s a living ecosystem. We’ll discuss the challenges of building and sustaining a library and the specific kind of community Atta Galatta continues to cultivate.
● The Money Match Behind Making it Happen: Perhaps the most vital piece of the puzzle—the resources. We’re getting into the brass tacks of the time, capital, and grit required to keep a cultural institution financially viable without losing its soul.
---
Event Details
● Host: Sathwik NN (Founder, Bangalore Finance Club)
● Guest: Subodh Sankar (Co-founder, Atta Galatta)
● Theme: The intersection of culture, community, and capital.
Whether you are an aspiring entrepreneur, a bibliophile curious about the "backstage" of your favorite haunt, or a finance enthusiast interested in the economics of niche businesses, this fireside chat offers a rare look at the grit behind the grace.
Come for the story, stay for the strategy.
Age: 16+

name: Visiting Katt and Fredda - Theatre Performance
description:
TheatreSHIft presents a rehearsed reading of Visiting Katt and Fredda (originally Besuch bei Katt und Fredda) by Ingeborg von Zadow, as part of our rehearsed reading series Not Lost in Translation.
What happens when the quiet world of two friends is interrupted by the arrival of a third? As Katt and Fredda are joined by an unexpected presence, their familiar dynamic begins to shift—making space for curiosity, discomfort, and subtle power struggles. Through this simple yet evocative situation, the play offers a glimpse into how relationships are tested and redefined.
Performed as a script-in-hand reading, actors fully embody the physical and emotional life of the characters while engaging with movement, space, and action. The performance evolves through subtle shifts in behaviour and power, building from quiet interaction into moments of tension, discovery, and emotional revelation, that resonates with both teens and adults.
This approach preserves the rawness of the text while exploring its performative possibilities, inviting audiences into a shared imaginative space—where what is spoken and unspoken holds equal weight.
Age: 12+

name: The Darker Moon - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Inviting everyone to a book reading by Syamanthaka, where she will read a few pages from her debut fiction novel – The Darker New Moon.
An IT professional, exploring her creative side, she would answer a few audience questions on how she self-published the book.
She would also be super happy to sign your copy of The Darker New Moon.
---
This event is open to all ages, although the genre itself is more suitable for “grown ups” who love reading a good mystery.

name: Art Journaling Workshop - Everybody Is An Artist Here
description:
Everybody's an artist and this is an afternoon for anyone who's been meaning to slow down, make something with their hands, and stop waiting until they're 'good enough' to try.
We're hosting an art journaling workshop at Atta Galatta — a guided, beginner-friendly session where you'll draw, collage, layer, and create a journal page that's entirely yours. no experience needed. no wrong answers. just you, some materials, and two hours away from your screen.
here's what the evening looks like:
🌿 warm-up exercises to shake off the perfectionism
🌿 guided journaling with mixed media (drawing, collage, textures)
🌿 reflection and optional sharing at the end
🌿 a curated takeaway envelope with mini art elements, just for you
📅 23rd May
🕓 1:00 PM – 3:30 PM
📍 Atta Galatta, Bangalore
🎟 ₹1399 per person (Extended Session + Takeaway included)
👥 Limited Slots — intentionally small :)
Age: All

name: The Bougainvillea Circle - Connection Circle
description:
"Sawubona" a Zulu Greeting meaning, "We See You". At Bougainvillea Circles you are offered a space to be heard, seen, welcomed as you are. A non-judgmental, safe, trusted space where you are free to be all that you want to be. Nothing More, Nothing less. No Restrictions, no judgements, just an intimate group of people looking for a space to be real. A space where we`re all less alone for a little while. Where we are seen, how we want to be seen. Conversations, Connections, Community of Compassion. All that makes the world go round, after Love of course!
Remember - Sawubona: We See You.
Age: 18+

name: Abstract Art Workshop - Workshop
description:
Hi, I'm Thomas, an artist, content creator and graphic designer. I'm hosting an abstract art workshop happening at Atta Galatta on the 23rd of May. It's a 2 hour event where I will be giving you a crash course of both the basic as well as advanced techniques of the Art form.
The workshop will also cover important information on the various avenues that can help you out with monetizing your own art pieces. You will experience just how therapeutic and calming, this form of art really is. I'll also give you some pointers on pricing, how to turn your art into merchandise and aspects about framing and sales. Come join us for a great experience!
Age: 18+

name: A Love Story of 12.5 Lacs - Solo Storytelling Act
description:
A software engineer chasing his acting dreams falls in love with a young aspiring actress. As they navigate auditions and struggles, their bond deepens—until one unexpected incident changes everything.
Without offering a real reason, she asks to break up, leaving him confused, heartbroken, and searching for answers.
Years later, during the COVID pandemic, a single message brings the past rushing back—with just one word: “Sorry.”
A poignant tale of love, silence, and unanswered questions that never truly fade.
Written and performed by Vaibhav Mishra, this solo act blends reality with fiction—promising moments that will feel deeply personal and relatable to everyone.
Age: 16+

name: Hired. Fired. Tired. - Book Launch
description:
Come join us for an afternoon of fun, banter, empowerment, and honest conversations on womanhood, workplace nightmares, women’s leadership, cognitive biases-and much more.
On May 24, join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Hired. Fired. Tired. by debut author Tushara Reddy- a candid and thought-provoking book exploring the realities of the modern tech workplace. Framed through the perspective of a first-generation millennial professional, the book unpacks the often-unspoken politics of the industry-where ambition, identity, and survival frequently collide.
Told from a woman’s point of view, Hired. Fired. Tired. speaks to those navigating toxic work cultures, imposter syndrome, layoffs, and the quiet pressures of being a newcomer. At once deeply personal and broadly relatable, the book gives voice to the silent struggles of everyday work life while imagining bold possibilities for a more humane and equitable tech future. Tushara also invites readers to be part of her personal journey of writing toward a more utopian tech world-one where empathy, fairness, and opportunity shape the future of work.
As a bonus, the evening will also feature a panel discussion with global tech leaders, exploring real stories, challenges, and ideas for building healthier workplaces in the tech industry.
Tushara Reddy is a mid-senior product manager with over nine years of experience across India, the UK, and the USA. An alumna of Queen Mary University of London, she currently lives in Bengaluru, where she balances work, workouts, and writing in her spare time.

Age: 18 and above

name: Meera and the Magic Kolam - Book Launch & Art Workshop
description:
Step into a playful, creative experience inspired by Meera and the Magic Kolam—a story that celebrates imagination, tradition, and self-expression.
This 2-hour interactive session introduces participants to the beauty of kolam, a traditional South Indian art form created using patterns of dots and lines, symbolizing harmony, rhythm, and everyday creativity.
The session includes a live storytelling and reading of the book, followed by a hands-on workshop where participants create their own unique kolam designs on canvas.
Each participant will also receive a copy of Meera and the Magic Kolam to take home.
Open to kids aged 5 and above, this workshop blends storytelling, art, and mindful creation—no prior experience needed.

name: Learning by Doing - A STEM Summer Workshop
description:
Bored during the summer break?  StE=mc^2 is back with a very exciting hands-on "Learning by Doing" Stem Summer workshop to explore how science works in real life and how we humans have discovered the secrets of the Universe - from the very tiniest - sub atomic realm to the largest - edge of the known Universe. 
Interspersed with scientific stories, films, books, puzzles, quizzes and much more - lets connect the dots through interesting Stem activities. Join us for a unique fun learning experience.
Age: 8+

name: Bollywood unplugged - An Evening of Music
description:
Shamiyana – The Music for Your Soul brings you a Bollywood unplugged experience that transcends time. From golden-era classics to contemporary chartbusters, every song is reimagined with a fresh, soulful twist—stripped down, intimate, and deeply evocative.
Set against the cozy, artistic vibe of Atta Galatta, this performance isn’t just a concert—it’s a journey through emotions, memories, and melodies that have shaped generations. Whether you’re a lifelong Bollywood lover or simply seeking music that resonates, Shamiyana promises an evening that lingers long after the last note fades.
Come, feel the music.
Age: All

name: Rhyme & Rasam: Poetry That’s Easy to Love - A Discussion Group
description:
Come, enjoy an evening of poetry with us. This is a guided reading with carefully-chosen poems. We won't torture the poems through over-analysis. We will examine them lightly and read them in context. 
You don't even have to love poetry to join. Come for some literary comfort and some surprises. Depth of flavour assured!
Hosted by writer and poet Suchi Govindarajan
Age: 16+

name: The Sarjapur Blues Band - A musical Evening
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an intimate and delightfully offbeat evening with The Sarjapur Blues Band, an underground, eclectic rhythm and blues duo known as much for their music as for their irreverent humour and refusal to play by the rules. With roots going back over three decades on Sarjapur Road, the band has built a quiet, word-of-mouth following, performing only their original music in homes, offices, and beloved cultural spaces.
The Sarjapur Blues Band—featuring Shekhar Seshadri on vocals and acoustic guitar, and Vinoo Matthew on electric guitar—will perform on 29th May, from 6 PM onwards. With over 50 years of performance experience between them, the duo brings a rare mix of lived-in musicality, sharp wit, and storytelling to the stage.
Known for their no-frills, no-recordings, no-internet presence, SBB remains refreshingly elusive in a world of constant visibility. Their performances are peppered with humour and candid banter, with songs like the “world-famous-in-Bangalore” Lady Nicotine offering a glimpse into their unique musical world.
Join us for an evening of music and the unmistakable charm of a band that has quietly become a local legend.
Age: All

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Food is more than sustenance; it is a map of our memories and a bridge to different cultures. This  month, we continue our dialogue on the narratives that connect what we read to what we eat. What does it mean to understand a civilization through its appetite? 
Book of the month: Hooked by Asako Yuzuki. Translated by Polly Barton
When: Saturday, 30th May, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots (link in bio)
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat!

Age: 18+

name: விசாரணை (The Trial)  - Book Discussion in Tamil
description:
What does it mean to live under a system that offers no explanation? What does it mean to find meaning when everything has been stripped away?
The 19th Sollazhi Reader's Circle brings together two essential texts that — across different centuries and very different registers — ask the same unsettling question: what sustains a human being when reason, justice, and certainty fail?
Franz Kafka's The Trial follows Josef K., who wakes one morning to find himself arrested — without charge, without cause, without any path to understanding. A century on, it remains one of the most precise maps of bureaucratic dread and institutional absurdity ever written.
Viktor Frankl's Man's Search for Meaning is an account of survival written from inside the Nazi concentration camps — and a philosophical argument for the indestructibility of the human will. Where Kafka names the wound, Frankl searches for what heals it.
Read together, these two books open a conversation about the limits of systems, the resilience of the self, and what it means to keep searching.
7 June 2026, Sunday | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM

Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar, Bengaluru
Organised by Sollazhi and Vishnupuram Ilakkiya Vattam, Bengaluru
Contact: 9535150062 | 9845086767 | 9986043446 | 9900324348

name: Lost in the Hyperbolic World - A Science Storytelling Session
description:
What if the world around you isn’t as straight as it feels?
This isn’t a science talk. It’s a story, one that slowly messes with how you see the world around you.
No mathematics. No equations. No prior knowledge needed.
Just a story that takes you into a hyperbolic world, where space doesn’t behave the way you expect, and leaves you wondering what was real and what wasn’t.
How would that look? How different would it be?
You may leave with a sense of what that world is like, and with more questions than answers.
Confusion to curiosity is the journey.
Age: 18+
